scope_note: Note 1(A): Ch.10 products are classified here ONLY if grains are present (in ear, on stalk, or loose grain). Note 1(B): Ch.10 does NOT cover hulled or otherwise worked grains — EXCEPTION: rice in all milled states (husked, milled, polished, glazed, parboiled, broken) stays in 10.06. Quinoa with pericarp removed to separate saponin (but no other processing) stays in 10.08. Note 2: 10.05 (maize) does NOT cover sweet corn → Chapter 7.

inclusions.description: Wheat and meslin: common wheat (Triticum aestivum — bread wheat), durum wheat (Triticum durum — pasta wheat, 28 chromosomes per Subheading Note), meslin (wheat+rye mix). Subheadings: durum wheat vs. other wheat (common/bread/soft). In grain form only
Rye, in grain form: rye (Secale cereale) — for bread, rye whisky, animal feed
Barley, in grain form: two-row and six-row barley — including malting barley and feed barley. Malted barley → Ch.11 (11.07)
Oats, in grain form: common oats (Avena sativa) — for rolled oats production, animal feed, oat flour
Maize (corn), in grain form: field corn/yellow corn, white corn — for food, feed, starch, ethanol. EXCLUDES sweet corn (Zea mays var. saccharata) → Chapter 7

Rice: paddy rice (with husk = rough/unhusked rice), husked rice (brown rice — husk removed), milled/semi-milled rice (polished/white rice), glazed or polished rice, parboiled rice, broken rice. Rice in ALL processing stages from paddy to milled grain stays in 10.06 per Note 1(B)
Grain sorghum: sorghum (Sorghum bicolor) — for human food, animal feed, syrup production in some markets

Buckwheat, millet and canary seed, and other cereals; quinoa: buckwheat (Fagopyrum esculentum), millet (various Panicum/Setaria/Eleusine species), canary seed (Phalaris canariensis), quinoa (Chenopodium quinoa — note: pericarp removal for saponin → still 10.08). Also: fonio, teff, Job's tears, wild rice (Zizania)

exclusions.condition: Grains that have been hulled, worked, rolled, flaked, pearled, chopped or milled into flour/meal (EXCEPT rice and saponin-treated quinoa)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 11 (milling industry products): rolled oats → 11.04; barley flour → 11.02; wheat flour → 11.01; buckwheat flour → 11.02; pearl barley → 11.03; oat flakes → 11.04. The only exception: rice in any form stays in 10.06. Note 1(B) is explicit — worked grains generally leave Ch.10

exclusions.condition: Sweet corn (Zea mays var. saccharata) — corn with high sugar content eaten as vegetable
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 7 (07.09 fresh sweet corn; 07.10 frozen sweet corn; 20.05 canned sweet corn). Note 2 to Ch.10 explicitly excludes sweet corn. Field corn/maize for grain → 10.05

exclusions.condition: Malted barley or malted wheat (kiln-dried, germinated grain)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 11.07 (malt, whether or not roasted). Germinated and kilned barley → 11.07. Green malt → 11.07. Roasted malt → 11.07 or 09.01 (if as coffee substitute). Raw un-malted barley → 10.03

exclusions.condition: Wheat/rice preparations for food (pasta, bread, pastry, noodles, cereal breakfast flakes)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 19: pasta/noodles of wheat semolina → 19.02. Bread/pastry → 19.05. Breakfast cereals (Corn Flakes, Rice Krispies type) → 19.04. Prepared rice dishes → Ch.19/21. The grain must remain in natural grain form to stay in Ch.10

classification_rules.rule: Note 1 — The 'grain must be present' rule: Ch.10 applies only while grain integrity is maintained
classification_rules.description: Chapter 10 headings require that GRAINS ARE PRESENT: the cereal must be in recognisable grain form — whole grain (with or without husk/hull), grain still in ear (on stalk), or grain separated from stalk but still intact as a grain. If the grain is BROKEN DOWN into smaller fragments (cracked, rolled, pearled, milled to flour/meal) → the product leaves Ch.10 and goes to Ch.11. EXCEPTION (Note 1B): RICE in any state from paddy to broken rice stays in 10.06 — the HS specifically grants rice flexibility.

classification_rules.rule: Rice heading 10.06: the complete rice processing chain exception
classification_rules.description: Rice is the UNIQUE cereal where all processing stages stay in ONE heading (10.06). The complete rice chain within 10.06: PADDY/ROUGH RICE (in husk, from field) → 1006.10. HUSKED RICE / BROWN RICE (husk removed, bran layer intact) → 1006.20. SEMI-MILLED RICE (partly milled, some bran removed). MILLED/WHITE RICE (fully milled, bran removed, white) → 1006.30. GLACÉ/GLAZED RICE (polished with talc or glucose). PARBOILED RICE (hydrothermal treatment before milling). BROKEN RICE (broken grains) → 1006.40. All stages → 10.06. Only when rice is further processed into rice flour (→ 11.02), rice starch (→ 11.08), rice noodles (→ 19.02), or rice puffed cereal (→ 19.04) does it leave 10.06.

classification_rules.rule: Durum wheat vs common wheat subheading: species and chromosome count
classification_rules.description: Heading 10.01 (wheat and meslin) has a major subheading split based on WHEAT SPECIES: DURUM WHEAT (Triticum durum) → 1001.11 (seed quality) or 1001.19 (other) — characterized by 28 chromosomes (tetraploid). Used primarily for semolina (for pasta). Subheading Note 1 defines durum wheat explicitly. COMMON/SOFT WHEAT (Triticum aestivum and similar hexaploid wheats, 42 chromosomes) → 1001.91/1001.99 — used for bread flour. MESLIN (mixture of wheat and rye) → 1001.xx. The variety distinction has significant tariff implications in many markets.

classification_rules.rule: Quinoa (10.08): pericarp removal exception similar to rice's Note 1(B)
classification_rules.description: Quinoa (Chenopodium quinoa) gets a special treatment similar to rice: if ONLY the PERICARP (outer seed coat) has been removed to separate the SAPONIN (bitter coating on quinoa seeds — must be removed to make quinoa edible) WITHOUT ANY OTHER PROCESSING → still in 10.08. 'Pre-washed' or 'rinsed' quinoa (saponin washed off but grain intact) → 10.08. Quinoa that has been flaked, steamed, or ground into flour → leaves Ch.10 (→ Ch.11). Puffed quinoa (for cereal bars) → Ch.19.

classification_rules.rule: Malted barley (10.03 vs 11.07): malting is the process threshold
classification_rules.description: Barley appears in two chapters based on MALTING: UNMALTED BARLEY (raw grain, undetermined use at time of classification) → 10.03. MALTED BARLEY (germinated then kiln-dried — the malting process transforms starch to fermentable sugars for brewing/distilling, creates enzymes) → 11.07. The malting process (steeping → germination → kilning) is what moves barley from Ch.10 to Ch.11. ROASTED MALT → still 11.07 (not 09.01 unless specifically sold as coffee substitute). Wheat malt (for wheat beer/whisky) → 11.07. Green malt (malted but not yet kilned) → 11.07.
